articles.title: US Treasury Secretary Bessent: Sometimes you have to 'escalate to de-escalate'
articles.expand: When asked in an NBS News interview on Sunday if President Donald Trump was “winding down” the war or “escalating” it, US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said, they are not mutually exclusive. Sometimes you have to escalate to de-escalate.”

articles.expand: Six central bank decisions and a hawkish Fed defined last week. Now flash PMIs, Australian CPI, and UK inflation data arrive against an active Iran conflict backdrop.

articles.title: BIS economist Shin Hyun-song named Bank of Korea governor
articles.expand: South Korean economist Shin Hyun-song, best known for predicting the 2008 Global Financial Crisis, was named on Sunday to head the central bank, facing inflation risk from the Iran war and uneven growth in Asia's fourth-largest economy.

articles.title: Pronóstico Semanal de Forex – USD/JPY, Petróleo Crudo Brent, Gasolina, Índice S&P 500 - 22 marzo 2026
articles.expand: La escalada de la guerra en Oriente Medio está impulsando los precios de la energía al alza y las acciones a la baja, mientras que los bancos centrales, en general agresivos, están elevando los rendimientos.

articles.title: 'Moat Investing' Surges As U.S. Investors Seek Stability Amid Volatility
articles.expand: With rising energy prices and cautious central bank policy contributing to heightened volatility, investors are revisiting fundamentals and prioritising businesses that can protect profits over time, rather than chasing short‐term gains.

articles.title: US Dollar Rises but Remains on Track for Weekly Loss Amid Inflationary Pressures
articles.expand: The US dollar rose on Saturday but remains poised to record weekly losses against major currencies. Investors have scaled back bets on Federal Reserve interest rate cuts, citing the potential for surging inflation driven by rising energy prices.

articles.title: USD/CHF flat as Fed outlook and Oil-driven USD demand limit downside
articles.expand: The Swiss Franc (CHF) trades flat against the US Dollar (USD) on Friday, reversing part of its earlier gains as the Greenback eases slightly from daily highs. At the time of writing, USD/CHF is trading around 0.7878 after briefly touching the 0.7900 mark.

articles.title: Asset Purchase Facility: Gilt Sales – Market Notice 20 March 2026
articles.expand: This Market Notice sets out the schedule for sales in Q2 2026 of gilts held in the Asset Purchase Facility (APF) for monetary policy purposes.

articles.title: Pound Sterling Price News and Forecast: GBP/USD trips down below 1.3350 as oil surges
articles.expand: The GBP/USD trims some of its Thursday’s gains, down by 0.84%, as traders turned risk-averse amid an escalation of the Middle East conflict and priced in no rate cuts by the Federal Reserve in 2026. At the time of writing, the pair trades below 1.3350 after hitting a daily high of 1.3442.

articles.title: USD strength vs Euro vulnerability: What’s next for the EUR/USD pair?
articles.expand: EUR/USD tests key level (1.1578) as the ECB's hawkish stance battles downside risks from Middle East conflict and spiking energy costs. The article analyzes the "April Hike" wildcard, NFP volatility, and critical technical levels. Outlook is split between technical recovery and fundamental risks.

articles.title: GBP/USD trips down below 1.3350 as Oil surges, Fed outlook boosts US Dollar
articles.expand: GBP/USD trims some of its previous-day gains on Friday, down by 0.84%, as traders turn risk-averse amid an escalation of the Middle East war and price in no rate cuts by the Federal Reserve (Fed) in 2026. At the time of writing, the pair trades below 1.3350 after hitting a daily high of 1.3442.

articles.title: AUD/USD falls as geopolitical tensions overshadow RBA rate hike
articles.expand: AUD/USD trades lower around 0.7040 on Friday at the time of writing, down 0.68% on the day, as the pair is pressured by a return of safe-haven demand despite supportive domestic factors in Australia.

articles.title: EUR/USD Weekly Forecast: War continues to steal the limelight, and not for good
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair bounced back in the last few days, settling for the week around 1.1530. The Iran war and central banks’ monetary policy announcements took centre stage, yet none was enough to impress speculative interest.

articles.title: EUR/GBP rises as markets assess ECB and BoE rate decisions, inflation outlook
articles.expand: EUR/GBP edges higher on Friday, recovering losses recorded in the previous day after the European Central Bank (ECB) and Bank of England (BoE) monetary policy announcements.

articles.title: USD/JPY rebounds as US Dollar strengthens, BoJ hawkishness tempers upside potential
articles.expand: USD/JPY trades around 158.70 on Friday at the time of writing, up 0.61% on the day, staging a clear rebound after Thursday’s sharp decline. The move comes as the US Dollar (USD) regains traction following a bout of volatility, with markets reassessing the outlook for US monetary policy.

articles.title: GBP/USD retreats as BoE hawkish shift meets resilient US Dollar
articles.expand: GBP/USD retreats on Friday, trading around 1.3380 at the time of writing, down 0.39% on the day, after Thursday’s strong rally following the Bank of England (BoE) decision.

articles.title: ECB's Makhlouf: Don't think ECB has a tightening bias
articles.expand: European Central Bank (ECB) policymaker and Governor of the Central Bank of Ireland Gabriel Makhlouf stated during European trading hours on Friday that the central bank doesn’t have a pre-determined rate path and the decision would be made meeting by meeting.

articles.title: Chart alert: Watch 157.40 on USD/JPY, hawkish BoJ, ECB and BoE ignite yen strength
articles.expand: USD/JPY weakens as hawkish signals from the European Central Bank and Bank of England offset Fed expectations, while a firmer tone from the Bank of Japan supports yen strength. A break below 157.40 could trigger further downside toward key support levels.

articles.expand: The BOE voted unanimously to keep Bank Rate on hold at 3.75% at its March 2026 meeting thanks to a sharp spike in global energy prices triggered by conflict in the Middle East.

articles.expand: The People's Bank of China rate setting is a non-event. China's main policy rate is now the reverse repo rate, currently at 1.4% for the 7-day. articles.title: BOC Holds Rates at 2.25% As US-Iran War and CUSMA Cloud Outlook
articles.expand: The Bank of Canada kept its policy rate unchanged at 2.25% for the fifth consecutive meeting, citing a deteriorating growth outlook and rising inflation risks stemming from the conflict in the Middle East.
